title: Home Based Parenting Education * African American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Home Based Parenting Education * African American Community 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community At Risk Families Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Domestic Violence Issues Ex-Offenders Hispanic/Latino Community TANF Recipients Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE TOGETHER WE CAN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Culturally specific home visiting support services that include parenting education, early detection of developmental delays and health issues, increase children’s school readiness and school success, and prevent child abuse and neglect. 901 NE Glisan Street Suite 250 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Parents who identify African and African American with a child ages 2 months-5 years Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Home Visits: Varies Email (503) 284-8000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT NORTH EAST HEALTH CENTER AND DENTAL CLINIC HEALTHY BIRTH INITIATIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home visits, health education, and support groups from pregnancy through the child's second year. Offers reduced-cost car seats, as well as certified assistance in installation. 5329 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Home visiting services: Pregnant African American women who are less than 18 weeks gestation. Those over 18 weeks gestation can still participate in support groups and classes. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6:30pm (503) 988-3387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE TOGETHER WE CAN MULTNOMAH COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT HEALTHY BIRTH INITIATIVE
